diagrams.title: Subspaces of R³
diagrams.description: Shows all possible subspaces in R³: point at origin (0D), line through origin (1D), plane through origin (2D), and full space (3D)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how subspaces appear geometrically - all must pass through origin

diagrams.title: Subspace Test Flowchart
diagrams.description: Decision diagram to check if a set W is a subspace
elements: If YES → Check: u+v ∈ W for all u,v?
If YES → Check: cu ∈ W for all c ∈ R?
If YES → W is a subspace
diagrams.purpose: Systematic approach to verify subspace property using ZAC conditions

diagrams.title: Sum and Direct Sum of Subspaces
diagrams.description: Shows U+W as union of spaces and U⊕W with no overlap except at origin
diagrams.purpose: Understand difference between sum (may overlap) and direct sum (unique decomposition)

diagrams.title: Span as Building Space
diagrams.description: Shows how individual vectors and combinations create spans
elements: Single vector v → line through origin
Two independent vectors → plane through origin
Three independent vectors in R³ → all of R³
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how span fills up space progressively with more vectors
